name: Multi-Source BFS (timed queue)
summary: Seed a queue with every rotten orange, then spread minute by minute; each fresh orange carries the minute it rots so the answer is the last minute reached.
visualizationIntent: init: draw the grid — empty cells dimmed, fresh oranges idle, rotten oranges visited; queue seeded with every rotten orange; count fresh oranges. loop: dequeue the front rotten orange (current). update: any fresh 4-neighbor rots (its cell flips to rotten/visited), fresh count drops, and it joins the queue carrying the next minute. move: advance the front. return: show the last minute reached, or -1 if fresh oranges remain unreachable.

lineExplanations.3: Record how many rows the grid has.
lineExplanations.4: Record how many columns each row has.
lineExplanations.5: Start an empty queue for the BFS wavefront.
lineExplanations.6: Start a counter of how many fresh oranges remain.
lineExplanations.7: Loop over each row to inspect its cells.
lineExplanations.8: Loop over each column in the row.
lineExplanations.9: Is this cell an already-rotten orange (2)?
lineExplanations.10: Seed the queue with this rotten orange at minute 0.
lineExplanations.11: Is this cell a fresh orange (1)?
lineExplanations.12: Count it — it still needs to rot.
lineExplanations.14: The four orthogonal moves — up, down, right, left.
lineExplanations.15: head marks the front of the queue — the next orange to process.
lineExplanations.16: Placeholder for the current orange's row; -1 means 'none yet'.
lineExplanations.17: Placeholder for the current orange's column.
lineExplanations.18: Keep spreading while the queue still holds rotten oranges.
lineExplanations.19: Look at the orange at the front of the queue.
lineExplanations.20: Advance the front marker (dequeue this orange).
lineExplanations.23: Read the minute at which this orange rotted.
lineExplanations.24: The answer is the latest rot-minute seen so far.
lineExplanations.25: Try each of the four directions from this orange.
lineExplanations.28: Is the neighbor an in-bounds fresh orange (1)?
lineExplanations.29: Rot it — set the cell to 2.
lineExplanations.31: Queue it to spread further, one minute later.
lineExplanations.32: After the spread stops, are any fresh oranges left?
lineExplanations.33: Some fresh orange was unreachable — return -1.
lineExplanations.34: All oranges rotted — return the minutes it took.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: is the class wrapper LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def orangesRotting(self, grid): defines the method; grid is the 2D list of 0/1/2 integers.
syntaxExplanations.4: grid[0] is the first row; len(...) gives the column count.
syntaxExplanations.5: [] creates an empty list used as a FIFO queue.
syntaxExplanations.7: for r in range(rows): loops r over each row index.
syntaxExplanations.8: for c in range(cols): loops c over each column index.
syntaxExplanations.9: grid[r][c] == 2 tests for a rotten orange.
syntaxExplanations.10: append([r, c, 0]) queues the cell with a minute stamp of 0.
syntaxExplanations.11: grid[r][c] == 1 tests for a fresh orange.
syntaxExplanations.14: A list of [row-delta, col-delta] pairs for the 4 directions.
syntaxExplanations.15: head = 0 starts the queue's front pointer.
syntaxExplanations.16: cur_r = -1 sets a sentinel; -1 is not a valid index.
syntaxExplanations.17: cur_c = -1 does the same for the column.
syntaxExplanations.18: while head < len(queue): runs while unprocessed oranges remain.
syntaxExplanations.19: queue[head] reads the front item [row, col, minute].
syntaxExplanations.20: head = head + 1 moves the front forward (dequeue).
syntaxExplanations.24: max(minutes, t) keeps the larger elapsed time.
syntaxExplanations.25: for d in dirs: loops over each direction.
syntaxExplanations.26: cur_r + d[0] gives the neighbor's row.
syntaxExplanations.27: cur_c + d[1] gives the neighbor's column.
syntaxExplanations.28: Chained 'and' checks bounds and that the neighbor is fresh (1).
syntaxExplanations.29: grid[nr][nc] = 2 turns the fresh orange rotten.
syntaxExplanations.30: fresh = fresh - 1 records that one fewer fresh orange remains.
syntaxExplanations.31: append([nr, nc, t + 1]) queues it to rot its neighbors next minute.
syntaxExplanations.32: if fresh > 0: checks whether any fresh orange survived.
syntaxExplanations.33: return -1 signals that not all oranges can rot.
syntaxExplanations.34: return minutes hands back the elapsed time.
